name: @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything
what_it_does: Anthropic's official 'everything' MCP server — a comprehensive test server that implements every MCP feature (tools, prompts, resources, sampling) for testing MCP clients and agent integrations.
use_cases: Testing that an MCP client correctly handles all protocol features
Debugging agent frameworks to verify tool call, prompt, and resource handling
Learning the MCP protocol by seeing all features in one server
Integration testing before connecting to production MCP servers
Benchmarking MCP client performance under various tool/resource loads
not_for: Production use — this is a test/reference server only
Real data operations — all responses are synthetic test data
best_when: You're building an MCP client, agent framework, or testing harness and need a comprehensive reference implementation to test against. The canonical correctness check.
avoid_when: You need actual functionality — this server only returns test/synthetic data.

auth.notes: No authentication required. Local stdio transport only.

pricing.notes: MIT licensed, free. Requires Node.js runtime.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Intentionally exercises all error conditions — designed for testing error handling. Returns structured test errors on demand.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All operations return deterministic test data.
known_agent_gotchas: All data is synthetic — do not use for real operations
Server deliberately exercises edge cases — behavior may be surprising to agents not designed for them

security_score_components.security_notes: For testing only — local stdio transport. No network exposure, no real data.
